sections.title: Executive Summary
sections.content:
Eight candidate markets survived constraint knockout (Step 08); all fall in the **investigate** band (5.0–7.5 composite), none clear the 7.5 pursue threshold. Three markets anchor the near-term portfolio: **Finfish Farming / RAS** (6.97) — whitespace with life-critical ODI (Opp>18 on flow-failure detection) and fragmented incumbents; **HVAC Contractors** (6.61) — customer-facing channel with short sales cycle and commissioning tool procurement; **District Energy / Steam & AC Supply** (6.30) — EU EED regulatory tailwind and multi-jet retrofit opportunity. Core risk is uniformly moderate feature fit (avg 5.14/10) — base commodity promise scores as *must_be*, not *delight*; differentiation depends on the integrated-temperature attractive feature and incumbent weakness exploitation. **Next step:** customer-discovery sprints in the top-3 markets to validate ODI interview targets, plus fast-track EN 1434 / MID certification to unlock the District Energy billing segment.

---

sections.content:

|---|---|
| Total markets evaluated (Step 02) | 8 |
| Markets eliminated by knockout (Step 08) | 0 |
| Markets surviving, ranked here | 8 |
| Pursue (>7.5) | 0 |
| Investigate (5.0–7.5) | 8 |
| Defer (3.0–5.0) | 0 |
| No-go (<3.0) | 0 |

sections.title: Per-market rationale & entry strategy
sections.content:
### #1 — Finfish Farming & Fish Hatcheries (NAICS 112511) · 6.97

**Rationale.** Top-ranked on incumbent vulnerability (8.40) — ~80% of RAS installations run with no real-time flow visibility; bucket-and-stopwatch or clogged turbine sensors dominate. High ODI (6.07) reflects life-safety opportunities: Opp=18 for flow-failure detection (dissolved-O₂ crash within 5–15 min), Opp=15–18 for per-tank flow visibility and temperature monitoring. Low-cost mitigable constraints (bubble tolerance, straight-pipe runs). Primary uncertainty: narrow commercial addressable market in EU/NA land-based RAS.

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| Entry strategy | 1) Target 2–3 flagship land-based RAS operators (e.g. Atlantic Sapphire, Salmon Evolution, AquaMaof installs). 2) Prove <60 s flow-failure detection under 2% bubble load in commercial trial. 3) Publish RAS case study (O₂ crisis prevention). 4) Partner with RAS system integrators (Billund, Krüger Kaldnes, AKVA). 5) Scale via OEM integration into new RAS builds. |
| Time to entry | 9–15 months |
| Investment | $250K–600K |

### #2 — Plumbing, Heating & A/C Contractors (NAICS 238220) · 6.61

**Rationale.** Strongest VN position (10.00) — contractor is direct buyer with short sales cycle and pricing power; customer-facing tool/instrumentation purchase, not component supply. Moderate incumbent vulnerability (5.60) — portable clamp-on meters (Testo, Flexim) dominate commissioning but are complementary (spot-check vs. permanent inline); Marquardt creates new category rather than displacing. ODI (5.29) supported by Opp=16 real-time zone-balancing response and Opp=14 thermal-energy metering during sign-off. Low constraint risk (9.50).

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| Entry strategy | 1) Target 5–10 German/DACH TGA service firms specialising in hydronic commissioning. 2) Prove <2 s flow-response latency on zone-valve adjustment vs. manual balancing valves. 3) Bundle as permanent inline add-on to EcoDesign commissioning kits. 4) Channel via wholesale (GC/Richter+Frenzel) and certified-contractor networks. 5) Cross-sell to smart-HVAC IoT platforms. |
| Time to entry | 6–12 months |
| Investment | $150K–400K |

### #3 — Steam & A/C Supply / District Energy (NAICS 221330) · 6.30

**Rationale.** VN hierarchy 9.50 (utility = direct asset buyer) plus highest ODI of the hydronic group (5.71) driven by Opp=15 billing accuracy and Opp=15 zero calibration drift. Strong regulatory tailwind: EU EED mandates per-building consumption metering; multi-jet impeller incumbents (70–80% share) require 5–6 year replacement cycles — clear lifecycle-cost displacement story. Constraint score 8.50 reflects three mitigable items (4th-gen/return-side targeting avoids temperature limit). EN 1434 / MID certification is the gating non-constraint barrier for the billing segment.

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| Entry strategy | 1) Target Danish/German district operators (Kamstrup, Danfoss substation OEMs, Fernwärme Wien) for pilot. 2) Prove zero-drift thermal-energy accuracy across 12-month control-loop trial (non-billing). 3) Submit for EN 1434 / MID module-B+D certification in parallel. 4) Upon cert., pitch multi-jet retrofit TCO case (zero-maintenance vs. 5-yr swap). 5) Scale via substation-package OEM channel. |
| Time to entry | 12–24 months (control loop); 18–30 months (billing-grade) |
| Investment | $600K–1.5M (includes certification) |

### #4 — Heating Equipment ex Warm-Air (NAICS 333414) · 5.96

**Rationale.** Best fit score of new markets (5.84) — boiler OEMs already integrate flow sensors; closest architectural adjacency (distance=2). ODI (5.33) supported by Opp=14 dry-fire prevention and EU EcoDesign thermal-output labelling. Incumbent vulnerability only 4.90 — Vortex (SIKA) is entrenched and already near-zero-pressure-drop, making direct displacement a price/feature parity fight. One mitigable constraint (straight-pipe runs) addressable at OEM design phase.

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| Entry strategy | 1) Target tier-2 European condensing-boiler OEMs (Vaillant sub-brands, BDR Thermea marques, Bosch Junkers private-label). 2) Prove pressure-drop + accuracy parity with SIKA VVX at equal unit cost. 3) Lead with integrated T + EcoDesign labelling data-out. 4) Supply-qualification cycle 6–12 months. 5) Scale via existing Marquardt OEM relationships. |
| Time to entry | 12–18 months |
| Investment | $400K–900K |

### #5 — Other Commercial & Service Industry Machinery (NAICS 333318) · 5.95

**Rationale.** Highest incumbent vulnerability (8.60) — turbine/paddle-wheel sensors clog in detergent/lint environments; 20% whitespace with no measurement; 0 dominant incumbent (fragmented +2.0 bonus). ODI 5.49 on Opp=15 water-consumption accuracy and WaterSense compliance billing. Offset by **weakest fit score (3.78)** — cost-sensitive OEM segment with low historical ultrasonic adoption; attractive features (integrated T) less valued. 4 mitigable constraints (NSF 61 cert. deferred by entering via laundry/car-wash first).

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| Entry strategy | 1) Target commercial laundry OEMs (Alliance, Electrolux Professional, Miele Professional) — supply-side warm-wash circuits only. 2) Prove zero-clog performance over 12-month MTBF vs. paddle-wheel baseline. 3) Expand to car-wash OEMs (Istobal, WashTec). 4) Pursue NSF 61 cert. for ice/beverage entry wave 2. 5) Position as WaterSense/IoT differentiator for OEM product-line managers. |
| Time to entry | 9–15 months |
| Investment | $300K–800K (plus $100K–200K NSF 61 deferred) |

### #6 — Air-Conditioning & Warm-Air Heating Equipment (NAICS 333415) · 5.91 — HOME

**Rationale.** Current production market (heat-pump and chiller OEMs). Highest constraint score (10.00 — already designed for envelope) but lowest new-market ODI (4.93) since needs are largely satisfied by existing incumbents including Marquardt's own deployed sensors. Fit 5.00 reflects mature competition (Vortex/SIKA dominant). Ranked for completeness — expansion potential is depth (new OEM accounts, higher DN range) rather than new-market discovery.

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| Entry strategy | 1) Defend & expand existing OEM accounts. 2) Upsell integrated-T variant for thermal-energy labelling. 3) Close SIKA VVX displacement gaps in tier-2 accounts. 4) N/A — existing channels. 5) Track as baseline. |
| Time to entry | 0–6 months (active) |
| Investment | <$150K (incremental) |

### #7 — Flowmeters (home industry reference, NAICS 41112501) · 5.50

**Rationale.** Not a market entry candidate — this is the product's own industry classification (commodity 41111926 rolls up here). Scored for traceability. Deep-component VN position (4.00) and low new-ODI (4.65) are expected; constraint score 10.00 trivially. **Exclude from go-to-market allocation.**

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| Entry strategy | Reference only — not a target market. |
| Time to entry | N/A |
| Investment | N/A |

### #8 — Breweries (NAICS 312120) · 5.34

**Rationale.** Weakest composite — low fit (4.93) and lowest incumbent vulnerability (4.50) because turbine sensors in glycol service are cheap, accepted, and already budgeted as consumables. ODI 5.34 supported by Opp=16 glycol-flow visibility and Opp=14 CIP solution verification, but the CIP position requires food-contact certification (medium cost, 6–12 months), pushing the addressable entry to the glycol-only loop. 3 mitigable constraints. Adjacent but not compelling versus markets #1–#4.

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| Entry strategy | 1) Target mid-size craft/regional breweries (50k–500k hl/yr) with multi-fermenter glycol loops. 2) Prove zero-maintenance advantage vs. turbine over 18-month brewery trial. 3) Enter glycol-only; defer CIP until FDA 21 CFR 177 cert. funded. 4) Channel via brewery-equipment integrators (GEA, Ziemann, Krones). 5) Scale via retrofit kits. |
| Time to entry | 12–24 months |
| Investment | $300K–700K (plus $50K–150K food-contact cert. deferred) |

---

sections.title: Component-score provenance
sections.content:
| Score | Source file(s) |
|---|---|
| odi_opportunity_score | `ODI_*.md` avg opportunity_score / 20 × 10 |
| feature_fit_score | `FIT_*.md` avg feature fit (tech-level, Step 09 only; no vendor step) |
| constraint_compatibility_score | `COMPAT_*.md` + `03_constraints.md` (10 − Σ penalties: low 0.5, med 1.0, high 2.0) |
| job_coverage_score | `JTBD_*.md` technology-specific coverage share × 10 (sensor actively serves ~3–5 of 8 Ulwick steps) |
| vn_hierarchy_score | `VN_*.md` strategic_position + depth_from_top adjustment |
| incumbent_vulnerability_score | `04_incumbents_home.md` + per-market VN incumbent notes (switching×0.4 + weakness×0.4 + fragmentation) |

---

sections.title: Key risks across the portfolio
sections.content:
| Risk | Exposure | Mitigation |
|---|---|---|
| No market clears *pursue* threshold (7.5) | All 8 | Staged pilots in top-3; re-rank after first customer wins |
| Feature fit averages only 5.14/10 | All | Lead with integrated-T attractive feature, not commodity flow |
| Food-contact / NSF / EN 1434 certifications gate billing segments | #3, #5, #8 | Parallel-track certification from month 0 |
| Vortex incumbent (SIKA) already zero-pressure-drop in #4/#6 | #4, #6 | Compete on integrated-T + digital diagnostics, not pressure drop |
| RAS addressable market depth | #1 | Validate unit-economics on top-10 EU/NA operators before scale-up |

---

rows.Mitigation: Compete on integrated-T + digital diagnostics, not pressure drop
Validate unit-economics on top-10 EU/NA operators before scale-up

entities.commodity_functional_promise: Non-invasive, zero-pressure-drop, zero-maintenance continuous volumetric flow + integrated temperature measurement for water-based hydronic media (DN15-DN25, <=90C, 1-10 bar).

ranked_markets.recommendation_rationale: Top-ranked on incumbent vulnerability (8.40) because ~80% of RAS installations operate with no real-time flow visibility, and ODI (6.07) reflects life-safety opportunities (Opp=18 for flow-failure detection, Opp=15-18 per-tank visibility). Two low-cost mitigable constraints only.
ranked_markets.entry_strategy_sketch: 1) Target 2-3 flagship land-based RAS operators (Atlantic Sapphire, Salmon Evolution, AquaMaof installs). 2) Prove <60s flow-failure detection under 2% bubble load. 3) Publish RAS case study. 4) Partner with RAS integrators (Billund, Krueger Kaldnes, AKVA). 5) Scale via OEM integration.

ranked_markets.recommendation_rationale: Strongest VN position (10.00) as the contractor is a direct buyer with short sales cycle. Low constraint risk (9.50). Incumbent vulnerability modest (5.60) since portable clamp-on meters are complementary rather than directly competitive; Marquardt creates a permanent-inline category adjacent to spot-check tools.
ranked_markets.entry_strategy_sketch: 1) Target 5-10 DACH TGA service firms in hydronic commissioning. 2) Prove <2s flow-response latency on zone-valve adjustment. 3) Bundle as permanent inline add-on to EcoDesign commissioning kits. 4) Channel via wholesale (GC, Richter+Frenzel) and certified-contractor networks. 5) Cross-sell to smart-HVAC IoT platforms.

ranked_markets.recommendation_rationale: VN hierarchy 9.50 (utility is direct asset buyer) combined with highest ODI of the hydronic group (5.71) on billing accuracy (Opp=15) and zero drift (Opp=15). EU EED regulatory tailwind and multi-jet impeller replacement cycles (5-6 years) create a clear TCO displacement case; EN 1434/MID certification is the gating barrier for the billing segment.
ranked_markets.entry_strategy_sketch: 1) Target Danish/German district operators (Kamstrup, Danfoss substation OEMs, Fernwarme Wien) for pilot. 2) Prove zero-drift thermal-energy accuracy in a 12-month control-loop trial. 3) Submit EN 1434/MID module-B+D certification in parallel. 4) Pitch multi-jet retrofit TCO once certified. 5) Scale via substation-package OEM channel.

ranked_markets.recommendation_rationale: Best fit score of the new markets (5.84) because boiler OEMs already integrate flow sensors; architectural distance=2 makes this the closest adjacency. Incumbent vulnerability only 4.90 since Vortex (SIKA) is entrenched and near-zero-pressure-drop, so differentiation relies on integrated-T and EcoDesign labelling rather than pressure-drop displacement.
ranked_markets.entry_strategy_sketch: 1) Target tier-2 European condensing-boiler OEMs (Vaillant sub-brands, BDR Thermea marques, Bosch Junkers private-label). 2) Prove parity with SIKA VVX on pressure-drop and accuracy at equal unit cost. 3) Lead with integrated-T + EcoDesign data-out. 4) Complete 6-12 month supply qualification. 5) Scale via existing Marquardt OEM relationships.

ranked_markets.market_name: Other Commercial and Service Industry Machinery Manufacturing
ranked_markets.recommendation_rationale: Highest incumbent vulnerability (8.60) because turbine and paddle-wheel sensors clog in detergent/lint environments and 20% of machines ship with no measurement at all; fragmentation bonus +2.0. Offset by the weakest fit score (3.78) due to cost-sensitive OEM segment and 4 mitigable constraints, including deferred NSF 61 certification.
ranked_markets.entry_strategy_sketch: 1) Target commercial-laundry OEMs (Alliance, Electrolux Professional, Miele Professional) on supply-side warm-wash circuits. 2) Prove zero-clog MTBF over 12 months vs. paddle-wheel baseline. 3) Expand to car-wash OEMs (Istobal, WashTec). 4) Pursue NSF 61 cert. for ice/beverage wave 2. 5) Position as WaterSense/IoT differentiator.

ranked_markets.market_name: Air-Conditioning and Warm Air Heating Equipment Manufacturing
ranked_markets.recommendation_rationale: Home market. Perfect constraint score (10.00) since the sensor was designed for this envelope, but the lowest new-ODI (4.93) because most needs are already satisfied by installed flow sensors (including Marquardt's own). Expansion potential is depth (new OEM accounts, higher DN range), not market discovery.
ranked_markets.entry_strategy_sketch: 1) Defend and expand existing OEM accounts. 2) Upsell integrated-T variant for thermal-energy labelling. 3) Close SIKA VVX displacement gaps in tier-2 accounts. 4) Reuse existing channels. 5) Track as baseline reference.

ranked_markets.recommendation_rationale: Not a market-entry candidate; this NAICS is the product's own industry classification. Deep-component VN (4.00) and low new-ODI (4.65) are expected. Retained for traceability only.
ranked_markets.entry_strategy_sketch: Reference only - exclude from go-to-market allocation.

ranked_markets.recommendation_rationale: Weakest composite: low fit (4.93) and lowest incumbent vulnerability (4.50) since cheap turbine sensors in glycol service are accepted and budgeted as consumables. CIP position requires food-contact cert. (medium, 6-12 months), pushing addressable entry to glycol-only loops.
ranked_markets.entry_strategy_sketch: 1) Target mid-size craft/regional breweries (50k-500k hl/yr) with multi-fermenter glycol loops. 2) Prove zero-maintenance vs. turbine over 18-month trial. 3) Enter glycol-only; defer CIP until FDA 21 CFR 177 cert. funded. 4) Channel via brewery-equipment integrators (GEA, Ziemann, Krones). 5) Scale via retrofit kits.

entities.executive_summary: Eight candidate markets survived constraint knockout; all fall in the investigate band (5.0-7.5 composite), none clear the 7.5 pursue threshold. Three markets anchor the near-term portfolio: Finfish Farming / RAS (6.97) - whitespace with life-critical ODI and fragmented incumbents; HVAC Contractors (6.61) - customer-facing channel with short sales cycle; District Energy / Steam & AC Supply (6.30) - EU EED regulatory tailwind and multi-jet retrofit opportunity. Core risk is uniformly moderate feature fit (avg 5.14/10) - differentiation depends on the integrated-temperature attractive feature and incumbent weakness exploitation. Next step: customer-discovery sprints in the top-3 markets plus fast-track EN 1434 / MID certification to unlock the District Energy billing segment.
